summary: Joel provides a brief news update covering two topics. First, he addresses reader questions about intern reading materials, noting the books are specific to Project Aardvark but pointing to his standard recommended reading list for programmers, which he hasn't updated since 2002 because the books are 'timeless classics.' Second, he offers a hint for a previous day's puzzle about government bonds, cleverly printed in white text to avoid spoiling it for those still working on it.
key_points: The intern book list was tailored to Project Aardvark specifically, not a general programming curriculum
Joel maintains a standard recommended reading list for programmers that he considers timeless enough to not need updating since 2002
The post includes an interactive puzzle hint about government bond prepayment, hidden in white text to preserve the challenge
money_quotes: I haven't updated it since 2002, but, you know what? These are timeless classics so I probably won't revisit it again for a few more years.
key_insight: The best programming books are timeless classics that remain relevant for years without needing to be updated.
